sentences.text: Hello.
Good team.
How are you?
God, how's it going over there?
well.
Nice.
I feel like we're actually starting fall season, maybe?
I feel like it was a really drastic jump.
We went from 88 to now mid-70s, windows open at night.
So, I don't know.
Bit of a change.
summer to be done, but I feel like it's here.
Yeah, it's crazy how quickly it happens.
I feel like this whole past year went by super quick.

Again, being in Seattle for the first time, there was really no snow, so it felt like a long fall, which is kind of nice, but at the same time, it was, like, around Christmas, it was kind of weird not having snow, so….
Yes.
this year.
That was kind of a… we had a really mild winter over here, too.
I think it maybe snowed a couple of times, but we're used to, like.
snow and, like, negative 30 degrees, and we didn't get any of that, so….
Weird.
One of those years, I guess.
Yeah.
Hmm.
How've we been?
What's new with you?
What's the name?
Yeah.
Yeah, I mean… I feel like every day something's happening.
Middle of budgets right now, … More acquisitions coming up at the end of the month.
some conferences in September and October, maybe November.
… some more acquisitions coming up end of September, so….
Yeah, lots of things happening, ….
Yeah, really just… Supporting the team with the transition, obviously.
… And then really firing on all cylinders, I mean, yeah.
It's been a busy summer, but I feel like it's just gotten busier, so… Yeah.
Maybe around Christmas, we'll see it die off a little bit, but….
Hopefully.
If it doesn't, that's okay, too, to figure it out.
I feel like this industry is pretty good with that, like, 2 weeks.

I'm always, like, trying to do a bigger trip around that time, because I'm like, I feel like no one's… no one's really wanting to meet, no one really seems like they're in the office, like, the week of Christmas and through the first week of January.
So, hopefully, you feel the same on your end.
Yeah, hopefully, hopefully we have a quiet Christmas season, but if we don't, then that's okay, too.
Yep.
Good to be busy.
Yeah.
Which, conferences are you going to this year?
Yeah, so there's a Yardi conference, yes.
I'm going to that in September, and then…
I believe Zillow Unlock as well?
Nice.
We're in October.
And yeah, debating if we're going to OpTech as a company, but if we do, then likely Optech as well, so….
Nice.
I've heard the Zillow conference is a fun one.
I've always….
I've heard good things.
Yeah.
So, we're gonna go this year, and we'll see.
Yes.
How about you guys?
Well, I will be at OpTech.
I think Patrick's going to Blueprint.
Have you ever been to that one?
Yeah, we have.
I've heard about it.
It's our fir… I'm not going, but it's our first year as a company going.
So Patrick and then someone from our ApartmentIQ side are going.
So, curious to see how that one goes.
But I think OPTEC, unless I'm missing any, I think Optech's the only one I have left for this year, as far as, like, industry conferences go.
We have a couple customer ones still, but….
Okay?
Yeah.
Should we try?
Cool, well, thanks, thanks for jumping on.
Obviously, we got your email about the renewal question, so I thought it would make sense to kind of combine that conversation with a check-in call.
Sure, that sounds good.
Awesome!
Alright, well, I have a very short deck for you today.
It's nothing too crazy, but, … yeah, definitely, I think, will be helpful as… as we talk about that.
Alright, so… let me get this here…
Okay.
So, like I said, quick agenda today.
Just wanted to check in with you.
I did provide updated reporting.
I think it's been now close to a full month since we last looked at that, and then we'll talk about the renewal pricing.
Cool.
Oh, I guess I skipped my What's New slide, but I know we kind of just talked about that, … What else is going on, like, on your end?
I know… I think we heard about Josh.
He's gone, right?
Is that true?
Yeah, yeah, I guess, yeah, so Josh is no longer with Widener.
Moving to North Carolina with his family.
Nice!
… Yeah, wishing the best.

Happy that him and his family are doing well, … do a whole bunch of things at once, like, he had his baby, and then was on FMLA, and then… decided to go back to North Carolina, so there's a few different things happening, ….
Yeah.
But yeah, it sounds like everything is good with him and his family, so that's awesome.
But, yeah, with Widener, … in terms of, like, rolling out new things, new initiatives, new strategies, I think the focus for the next year or two is really going to be operational maturity.
I think we've been onboarding and testing out a lot of new things, finding what works, what doesn't, and trying to catch up in a lot of ways.
… Just to, like, a really quickly evolving, now, industry.
So, we're not looking to really roll out anything or do anything too crazy.
I think it's just refining the systems we have, improving just processes, becoming more efficient, and really just dialing in what we basically have in motion right now, so….
Yeah.
I wouldn't say anything crazy, ….
In terms of, like, brand new softwares, or brand new… You know, strategic changes or, like, large, like, even, like, org chart, like.
We're not doing, like, a mass hiring or, like, moving around people, like, it's….
Yeah.
Josh, Josh is out of the picture right now.
Team is how the team is, and we're just looking to basically develop operational maturity, refining kind of what we already have in place.
Okay.
Are you looking to replace Josh's position, or are you just kind of going with what you have now, team-wise?
Yeah, so we're not looking to backfill his position, … So, as of, yeah, right now, unofficially, I'm overseeing the marketing team.
Okay.
Nice.
That's cool for you!
Congrats on that!
Thank you, … Yeah, that's, like, full transparency, like, that was kind of the idea of me coming to the U.
S.
Okay.
was eventually to… to move into that position and to replace him, as he moved on to the next position.
… Yeah, I guess technically that happened, I guess.
Just in a bit of a different order than I thought.
That being said, like, technically, unofficially, but just right now, overseeing, the marketing team and its functions, and … Yeah, reporting directly to Peter now.
Nice!
Congrats, that's exciting.
Thank you.
Yeah, it's been a journey, that's for sure.
Yeah.
I'm sure there's a lot more going on now at this point.
Are you gonna be the new Josh on panels at this… at these conferences, and getting out there?
I mean, if I get the offer, yeah, I guess so.
No, that'd be a fun thing to get into, ….
I think right now the biggest thing is just supporting The team during the transition, making sure everybody's supported and happy and engaged, and ….
Yeah.
And then from there, seeing what's next, I guess.
Okay.

Well, yeah, I won't be at OpTech, which I'm sad about, but speaking on behalf of Kylie, which would probably say the same thing, I'm sure if your team does go, I know we'd love to meet more of your team in person, and hang out with you guys, and… Meet up.
Yeah.
That'd be awesome.
Yeah, I think if we do go, it'll be a bit of a larger group as well, so that would be positive.
I think some more operational people as well.
Awesome.
Well, let me know if you guys decide to go for sure.
I know I'll be there, so, if there's any time for a team happy hour, lunch, or coffee, or anything you guys can fit in, I'd love to.
Cool, sounds good.
Sweet.
Okay, well, that's really good to know.
We can move over to reporting now.
So I did… I put in… This kind of looks like a lot, but I put in a new report.
So the last time we talked, we were looking at… it was like a 6-month report.
Added on the month of July, which actually made a pretty big jump from, the last 6 months, which is a great thing.
So you'll see the date on the top.
It's basically year-to-date.
We've been live a little longer than that, but I wanted to just compare this year with the same period last year.
And then what I….
What's up?
The year-to-date would be 2026, right?
I just want to make sure.
I think it says 2025.
Yeah, so this is January of this year to July this year compared to the same period of last year.
Okay, okay.
Yep.
And then what I highlighted here, so… these increases, like, they obviously all look really good, but what I wanted to try and compare with was exactly the same report that we looked at last month.
So, these percentage changes are actually the increases that we're seeing just from the last 6 months to now adding a 7th month in.
So, everything is up over 10% in just the last, like, 30 days in combination, which is really awesome.
… Including ones that were previously down.
So, phone actions were… they were still, like, in the negative, and then I believe also, I think it was website actions were both red when we looked at it last year.
Website actions are now up, phone actions are… Close to being up, but up 12% since the last call, so….
Okay, sorry to clarify, the, …
Like, the block numbers, like the 10%, 14%, 12%, that's month over month, and then the other ones are period over period.
So the… these green numbers, basically what I did is I looked at our last report, which was January through June, compared to the same year prior, and then I pulled out, and then I added July.
Hello.
Okay, so you have the first period's January to June, 6 months, the second period is January to July, 7 months.
But now we're 7 months in, and this is the difference between the 6-month period versus now the 7-month period.
Okay.
Yeah.
Nice.
Yeah, so looking really good, … I know this… this kind of data is the most helpful for you guys, and we can continue to provide whatever type of reporting you want.
After this call, I will send you the full report that has the per property breakdown, … So that you can see that, and it also includes some additional data around, like, mobile versus desktop.
impression increases, it will show a graph, of all these KPIs over time, and then it also will include some search impression data.
Cool.
No, this looks good.
And again, it's kind of difficult to track specifically what Rentable touches, but I think with these kind of features, or like, yeah, softwares.
Because they impact organic traffic, we want to see a general overall lift to organic traffic, leads and leases.
then we can look at the ecosystem and see, did this have a positive influence, potentially?
So these are all positive metrics pointing towards that.
And from what I can tell on my end, looking at the whole portfolio, not the property specifically we've been dealing with, we are seeing a positive trend year over year.
So again, you know, correlation, not necessarily causation, but, with this, with social media, it's kind of how you have to look at it, is the overall bucket of leads and leases and traffic.
So this is going the right direction, and those metrics are going the right direction as well.
For sure.

What specifically… if… if anything, could we send over that would be helpful for you to, like, I don't know, differentiate between the properties that are on, or just, I guess, what overall are you looking at when it comes to, like, getting closer to the end of the year, and how you guys want to move forward?
Oh, for sure.
So when we do, like, an actual analysis, we're looking directly at the properties that we have on rentable, and looking at the year-over-year changes in various metrics.
Right now, I'm looking at the whole portfolio as a quick, just, like, once overview.
Obviously, it's not the whole portfolio, but it's showing that things are moving in the right direction.
When we do, like, a full analysis to look at these properties that we have on for the long term, what are those impacts, we can compare these metrics to our own metrics and see.
how they do.

And again, we can't attribute everything perfectly, but if we have these properties, we're looking at these metrics, and we look at our own internal metrics, we should ideally see leads and leases increase year over year, as well as other metrics as well.
So, that's the idea.
Okay, that's super helpful.
So, looking to see… More than just leads and leases, but that's definitely a big part of the chunk of data that you guys are going to want to compare.
Yeah, so I would say fundamentally, like, what we do is… the whole point of all this is really to drive high-quality, cost-efficient traffic.
And we can't always track and measure every single part of that puzzle, but whatever we're putting in, we have to see what the output is improving.
And ideally, we can track each of those things, the things that we can't.
we have to try to look at some sort of trend going the right way.
So for us, leads and leases is the most important thing.
I mean, yeah, because impressions, actions.
whether phone or website, maps, whatever.
All those things moving the right direction should then impact conversion rates.
lease, volume, lease volume on the back end, but that should… this should go to here, kind of thing, right?
That being said, so far, things are looking like they're going that direction.
I think that's why we needed a bit more time, just to make sure that we could actually see what the impact to the business was on those properties.
But when we go and do the analysis, that's what we'll be looking at, is the GA4 metrics.
How they compare to properties who weren't on it, but also looking at leads and leases, generally speaking, Inside of Yardi, inside of our system.
Okay, so you're gonna look… when you do that, you're gonna take… because I know a majority of your properties are not using this product, right?

So… … obviously, our group of properties is a smaller subset of data, but I think, if I'm hearing you right, like, you're gonna look at the… not just the KPIs on Google, but also, like, the lead and lease conversions for just this small group of properties, and then are you gonna compare it to all of the other properties, like, increases?
So last time what we did is we chose similar properties in those submarkets, or at least similar enough sub-markets, both class type and unit count, just to have a general comparison.
Again, these aren't perfect comparisons.
We want to be fair, and make sure we're not just kind of testing within a bubble, but we're testing against something.
So, yes, it's GA4 metrics.
Those JA4 metrics, whatever they are, should have an impact to the bread and butter of what we do, which is leads and leases.
We also want to make sure we're comparing not just to that one set, but also to another set of data that's very similar A control group, basically.
Okay, that's helpful to know.
Got it.
So you'll try and match, maybe-ish, like, a one-to-one with the properties on that are similar to properties not.
Yeah, if we can, and again, it's a bit loose.
We're not going to be like, oh, this is, like, a 0.
5% better.
It's just, like, a general kind of trend comparison.
And again, it's just building out the case of like, I love this platform, I love what it does, but does it actually have an impact to our bottom line?
And it's just building out that story.
Okay.
Okay, that's very helpful.
Kylie, do you have any questions on that?
The only thing I'm thinking is, do you know, like, what sample of time is going to be the best to be able to track all of this and, like, get, like, a really good picture of what this does?
Yeah, so it depends on how many months of data we have, … we like to do 12, like, year-over-year, but trailing 12-month comparisons, with lease data.

With lead data, it can be 3-month comparisons, because we're doing 3-month… Like, quarterly meetings with our teams, but for these larger trends, we're probably looking at, like, a 12-month trailing data set and comparing year over year.
Okay, yeah.
And this, Morgan, this was rolled out at the beginning of January, right?
Yes.
overhauled.
Right at the end of the year, we should have a better idea of, like, a general… even, like, 11-month trailing.
Take that time period and compare it to the same type period the year before, and then compare all those metrics as well, and the control group.
Okay, yeah.
And do you guys have the flexibility, as far as, like, budgeting goes, to, like, wait until closer to the end of the year to make that decision?
So, for certain things, like, budgets are happening right now.
That's why I wanted to get a general idea of rates, even if it's not set in stone, just to basically have that for budgets.
But then in terms of contract negotiations, as long as it's before Well, really, it would have to be… like, latest… like, late October, early November.
… But again, the point of this call was just to kind of get an idea of, like, general, kind of.
budgeting purposes for the rates, and then we can have a discussion later on in the fall.
Okay.
Okay, all super helpful, so we'll see where we're at by, like, mid-late October, as far as the data goes.

Yes, we'll give you all of the pricing that you guys need now, just so you can budget it in if you want to, and then I'll be out, but Kylie will obviously make sure That we can just keep providing kind of a similar reporting set for you, so that by the time we hit October, hopefully you have a good enough idea of what's… What direction you're leaning, but obviously you have until the end of the year, so….
Yeah, sounds good.
Okay.
Alright….
Well, hopping into renewal rates.
So, obviously the current price is that $150 per property per month.
We are holding that price for Widener as you continue to bring properties on.
So there will be no increase in price property by property, but we are still offering those price breaks for getting a bulk amount of properties on.

So, $150 per property if we continue to scale property by property, and then if we do get to a point where we are looking at a larger rollout, we're still honoring the $99 per property for 100 or more properties, and then one additional price break if we're looking at over $200.
So, over 200 properties with another price break down to $85.
Okay.
Any questions on that?
No, that's basically the same as last time as well.
So yeah, I was just mostly checking with the, like, the current rate, if that was going up at all year over year, but it sounds like it's staying the same, which is good news, so….
Yeah.
Yeah, we wanted to keep it the same.
We didn't want to complicate things, especially as we're still more in, like, a pilot phase.
We thought, let's hold that rate, let's not, make any changes to that until we get to, like, a larger rollout, so….
Sure.

And then, while we're on this slide, I also wanted to ask, I know after our conversation with Lauren, we talked a bit about the social product, and I obviously know that you guys are still in with Sochi, but we talked about looking at kind of like a custom model for you guys, where we posted 4 times, I think that's supposed to be per month.
4 times per month versus the 8 per month for a discounted price.
Of 49 a property.
Have you guys had any other conversations about that?
So yeah, those conversations are still happening internally.
No additional updates right now.
Okay, yeah, sounds good.
Cool.
Okay, … Do you want to… I don't know, just timeline-wise, I guess, for the social conversation as well, do you want to reconnect, like, early-mid September on that?
Do you think that would… be helpful to get back on a call if you want.
We can also bring Lauren back in, if you need.
Yeah, so I think right now, we're piloting a few different options and getting an idea of just kind of what the next steps are.
Not piloting, but sorry, like, compiling a list.
So once we have that, they'll be reaching out to decide on next steps, so that's with Lauren and her team right now.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Cool.
If they need anything from us, … If they want to, like, test a property, I don't know.
Whatever would be helpful, just let us know, and we'll keep those conversations going.
Cool, sounds good.
Alright, that's honestly all we got for today.
I didn't think this would take the full time.
… Actually, I did have… I had one more slide on here that I guess I can just show you while we're here.

It's pretty much exactly what we just talked about, but it might just help, … it's just kind of gonna show you, like, how we would actually contract this together based on what you ended up deciding on.
So… I did want to ask you one question.
So, your current contract, the start date was December 1st through December of this year, so technically.
it would renew on December 1st of this year rather than January, but if you would rather us move that to a January 1, we can do that.
And basically, we would just extend the contract you have now through the end of the year, and then everything new would take place in January.
That might be ideal, just so everything falls from the same time period.
Yeah.
Yeah, if you guys wouldn't mind, that'd be awesome, actually.
Okay, I'll make a note of that, that shouldn't be an issue at all.

And then… We'll do, yeah, same terms, and then obviously the number of properties, the addition of social, that would just determine the custom pricing you would get, and then the way we would send it over, it'll just show one full monthly rate, similar to how it is now, but we'll just add, like, an Exhibit A sheet that will break down every property and all the information there, just so you only have to sign one agreement.
Okay, yeah, sounds good.
Any questions on that?
Should be good.
Everything I need for right now.
Yeah, I appreciate it, thank you.
Awesome.
Okay, you're welcome.
Let us know if anything else comes up, but I'll send over a quick recap.
Like I said, I'll do… I'll send over the full report as well, and if you have any questions on it, just let me know.
Sounds good.
Alright, see you guys.
Thanks!
Good evening!
Yep, yep.
